title: Responsive web self-service access
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to access self-service transactions through a responsive web interface, so that I can perform HR-related tasks from any device browser without needing a dedicated app.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am an authenticated employee, when I open the self-service portal in a web browser, then the interface renders responsively across desktop, tablet, and common mobile screen sizes and orientations.; Given I access the portal, when Phase I is in scope, then all self-service transactions are available via the responsive web interface only, with no dependency on a native mobile application.; Given a native mobile application is requested, when it is evaluated against release scope, then it is deferred to Phase II and is not part of this release.; Given I use different browsers or devices, when I load the portal, then layout and functionality remain consistent and usable.
source: FR 6.16.1 (responsive web interface; mobile is Phase II)

title: View authorized personal and employment information
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to view my authorized personal and employment information, so that I can confirm my records are accurate and up to date.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am an authenticated employee, when I open my self-service profile, then I can view the personal and employment information I am authorized to see.; Given information exists that I am not authorized to view, when I access my profile, then that information is hidden and not displayed to me.; Given my authorization scope is defined by role and policy, when I view my information, then the visible fields reflect exactly what my role and policy permit.
source: FR 6.16.2, FR 6.16.4 (view authorized personal and employment information under role/policy control)

title: Manage authorized personal and employment information
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to manage my authorized personal and employment information, so that I can keep my own records current without routing every change through HR.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am an authenticated employee, when I edit a field I am authorized to manage, then the update is validated and saved to my record.; Given a field is outside my authorized scope, when I attempt to change it, then the change is not permitted and the field is read-only or blocked.; Given an update requires approval by policy, when I submit a change, then it is handled according to the applicable policy workflow before taking effect; Given I enter invalid data, when I attempt to save, then the system rejects the change with a validation message and does not persist it.
source: FR 6.16.2, FR 6.16.4 (manage authorized personal and employment information under role/policy control)

title: Access profile information via self-service
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to access my profile information through self-service, so that I can review my details in one place.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am an authenticated employee, when I open self-service, then a profile information section is available to me.; Given I open the profile section, when it loads, then it displays my authorized profile details.; Given my role or policy restricts certain profile data, when the profile section loads, then only the permitted profile details are shown.
source: FR 6.16.3, FR 6.16.4 (self-service access to profile information)

title: Access leave functions via self-service
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to access leave functions through self-service, so that I can review balances and act on my leave.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am an authenticated employee, when I open self-service, then leave functions are available to me.; Given I open the leave section, when it loads, then it displays the leave information and actions I am entitled to based on my role and policy.; Given a leave function is not permitted by policy, when I open the leave section, then that function is hidden or blocked.

title: Access announcements via self-service
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to access announcements through self-service, so that I stay informed of organizational communications.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am an authenticated employee, when I open self-service, then an announcements section is available to me.; Given announcements are published for me, when I open the announcements section, then I can view them.; Given an announcement is targeted to a different audience, when I open announcements, then it is not shown to me.

title: Access documents and feedback functions via self-service
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to access documents and feedback-related functions through self-service, so that I can retrieve my documents and participate in feedback activities.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am an authenticated employee, when I open self-service, then documents and feedback-related functions are available to me.; Given I open the documents section, when it loads, then I can access the documents I am authorized to view.; Given a document is outside my authorized scope, when I browse documents, then it is not accessible to me.; Given I open a feedback-related function, when it is available to me by role and policy, then I can perform the associated feedback action.
source: FR 6.16.3, FR 6.16.4 (self-service access to documents and feedback-related functions)

title: Role and policy controlled self-service access
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want self-service access to be governed by role and policy, so that employees only see and act on functions permitted to them.
acceptance_criteria: Given a user attempts a self-service action, when access is evaluated, then it is granted or denied based on the user's role and applicable policy.; Given a function is not permitted by the user's role or policy, when the user accesses self-service, then that function is hidden or blocked.; Given roles or policies change, when a user next uses self-service, then their available functions and editable fields reflect the updated role and policy configuration.; Given both viewing and managing are governed, when access is evaluated, then read and edit permissions are each enforced per role and policy.

title: Access attendance functions via self-service
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to access attendance functions through self-service, so that I can review and act on my attendance information.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am an authenticated employee, when I open self-service, then attendance functions are available to me.; Given I open the attendance section, when it loads, then it displays the attendance information and actions I am entitled to based on my role and policy.; Given an attendance function is not permitted by policy, when I open the attendance section, then that function is hidden or blocked.

title: Non-user employees excluded from self-service
user_story: As an Employee (Non-User), I want my HR records to be maintained on my behalf, so that I am covered by the system even though I have no self-service login.
acceptance_criteria: Given an employee is designated as a Non-User, when self-service access is provisioned, then no self-service login is granted to that employee.; Given a Non-User employee exists, when their transactions must be performed, then they are handled by HR or an authorized admin rather than through employee self-service.; Given a Non-User is later converted to a User, when access is granted, then they gain role- and policy-controlled self-service access consistent with other users.
source: FR 6.16.2, FR 6.16.4 (self-service is user- and role/policy-scoped; non-users have no portal access)

title: Configure self-service authorization scope
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to configure which self-service functions and fields employees can view and manage, so that authorized scope aligns with organizational policy.
acceptance_criteria: Given I administer self-service, when I define role- and policy-based permissions, then those permissions determine which sections, functions, and fields are visible and editable to employees.; Given I restrict a field to view-only, when an employee opens self-service, then that field is displayed but not editable.; Given I remove access to a function, when affected employees next open self-service, then that function is hidden or blocked; Given I change authorization configuration, when it is saved, then it applies to subsequent self-service sessions without dropping any previously permitted access unintentionally.
source: FR 6.16.2, FR 6.16.4 (authorized information scope; role and policy controlled access)

title: Persist self-service changes to the bitemporal canonical record
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want self-service changes to personal and employment information to be written to the canonical bitemporal data model, so that every change is effective-dated and its history is preserved.
acceptance_criteria: Given an employee submits an approved self-service change to their personal or employment information, when it is persisted, then it is written to the canonical record as a new effective-dated version rather than overwriting prior data.; Given a change takes effect, when the record is queried as-of any date, then both the valid-time (when the change applies) and the transaction-time (when it was recorded) are retained.; Given a prior value existed, when a new version is written, then the earlier version remains available as history and can be reconstructed.; Given a self-service edit is reverted or corrected, when it is saved, then the correction is recorded as a new version and the original entry is not physically deleted.
source: FR 6.16.2 (L1 domain/data: effective-dated, bitemporal persistence of self-service changes)

title: Tenant-scoped self-service data isolation
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want self-service reads and writes to be confined to the employee's own record within their tenant, so that data is isolated and no cross-tenant or cross-employee access can occur.
acceptance_criteria: Given an authenticated employee opens self-service, when their personal, employment, leave, attendance, document, or feedback data is retrieved, then only records scoped to their tenant and their own employee record are returned.; Given a request would resolve to another tenant's or another employee's data, when it is evaluated at the persistence layer, then row-level security prevents the access regardless of the requesting UI or API.; Given a self-service write is submitted, when it is persisted, then it is committed only within the employee's own tenant-scoped record.; Given tenant isolation is enforced, when access is logged, then the access is attributable to the correct tenant and employee.
source: FR 6.16.2, FR 6.16.4 (L1 domain/data: tenant-scoped storage and row-level security)

title: Configure profile fields and UDFs exposed to self-service
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to define the profile and employment field schema, including custom fields (UDFs), that self-service exposes for view and edit, so that the portal reflects our field configuration without code changes.
acceptance_criteria: Given I maintain the self-service field configuration, when I add or modify a field or user-defined field (UDF) in the metadata schema, then it becomes available to be shown or edited in self-service according to its configuration.; Given a field is marked view-only or editable in config, when an employee opens the relevant section, then the field behaves per the configured mode.; Given field configuration is versioned and effective-dated, when I publish a change, then it applies from its effective date to subsequent self-service sessions without altering historical records.; Given validation rules are defined in config for a field, when an employee edits that field, then those rules govern acceptance of the value.
source: FR 6.16.2, FR 6.16.3 (L2 config/metadata: profile/UDF field schema and validation driving self-service)

title: Self-service change requests routed through the approval engine
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want my self-service changes that require approval to be routed through the workflow engine, so that they are reviewed and take effect only once approved.
acceptance_criteria: Given I submit a self-service change to a field that policy marks as approval-required, when it is saved, then the Workflow/Approval engine creates a request and routes it to the configured approver graph.; Given the request is pending, when I view the affected field, then it reflects the pending state and the prior value remains in effect until approval.; Given an approver acts on the request, when it is approved, then the change is applied to my record, and when it is rejected, then the change is discarded and my record is unchanged.; Given the approver graph is defined in configuration, when routing occurs, then the engine follows that configured graph without hard-coded approver logic.
source: FR 6.16.2, FR 6.16.4 (L3 engine: Workflow/Approval engine executes self-service change approvals)

title: Dynamic self-service forms rendered and validated by the forms engine
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want self-service view and edit screens to be generated by the forms engine from field metadata, so that the fields, layout, and validation always match the current configuration.
acceptance_criteria: Given I open a self-service section, when the screen renders, then the Forms/Dynamic-Fields engine builds it from the configured field schema, including any UDFs, rather than from a fixed hard-coded form.; Given a field has a configured data type and validation rule, when I enter a value, then the engine validates it against that rule and blocks invalid input before submission.; Given the field configuration changes, when I next open the section, then the rendered form reflects the updated fields and rules without a code release.; Given a field is configured as view-only for my role, when the form renders, then the engine presents it as non-editable.
source: FR 6.16.2, FR 6.16.3 (L3 engine: Forms/Dynamic-Fields engine renders and validates self-service forms)

title: Announcements delivered via the notification engine
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want announcements to be delivered to me through the notification engine using configured templates and audience targeting, so that I receive the communications intended for me.
acceptance_criteria: Given an announcement is published to an audience that includes me, when the Notification/Template engine evaluates targeting, then the announcement is delivered to my self-service announcements section.; Given an announcement is targeted to a different audience, when the engine evaluates targeting, then it is not delivered to me.; Given a notification template is configured, when the announcement is rendered, then its content follows the configured template.; Given I have unread announcements, when I open self-service, then the engine surfaces them so I can view them.
source: FR 6.16.3 (L3 engine: Notification/Template engine delivers targeted announcements)
